ddls:
CREATE TABLE "games" (
  "game_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each game */,
  "game_name" TEXT /* Name of the game */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the game */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the game (e.g., Action, Adventure) */,
  "developer" TEXT /* Name of the game developer or development team */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Name of the game publisher */,
  "platforms" TEXT /* Platforms on which the game is available */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average user rating of the game */,
  "num_players" INTEGER /* Number of players who have played the game */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("game_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each level */,
  "game_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the game this level belongs to */,
  "level_number" INTEGER /* Number of the level within the game */,
  "difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard) */,
  "estimated_completion_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time (in minutes) to complete the level */,
  "required_items" TEXT /* List of items required to complete the level */,
  "is_boss_level" INTEGER /* Indicates if the level is a boss level */,
  "num_enemies" INTEGER /* Number of enemies in the level */,
  "num_collectibles" INTEGER /* Number of collectibles available in the level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_levels_game_id FOREIGN KEY ("game_id") REFERENCES games ("game_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the level the metrics pertain to */,
  "attempts" INTEGER /* Total number of attempts made by players */,
  "wins" INTEGER /* Total number of wins achieved by players */,
  "p_win" REAL /* Win rate calculated as wins/attempts */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Error rate calculated as errors/attempts */,
  "average_time" REAL /* Average time (in seconds) it takes for players to complete the level */,
  "max_attempts" INTEGER /* Maximum number of attempts made by a single player */,
  "avg_attempts" REAL /* Average number of attempts made by players */,
  "max_wins" INTEGER /* Maximum number of wins by a single player */,
  "avg_wins" REAL /* Average number of wins by players */,
  "most_common_error" TEXT /* Most common error message encountered by players */,
  "player_segment" TEXT /* Segment of players (e.g., novice, intermediate, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_performance_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "player_segment" TEXT /* Segment of players (e.g., novice, intermediate, advanced) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "total_playtime" INTEGER /* Total playtime (in minutes) across all games */,
  "num_games_played" INTEGER /* Number of games played by the user */,
  "num_levels_completed" INTEGER /* Number of levels completed by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_progress" (
  "progress_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each progress record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making progress */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the level being completed */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date when the level was completed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the level (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  "time_spent" INTEGER /* Time spent (in seconds) on the level */,
  "num_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of attempts made by the user to complete the level */,
  "num_wins" INTEGER /* Number of wins achieved by the user on the level */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of errors encountered by the user on the level */,
  "last_attempt_date" TEXT /* Date of the last attempt to complete the level */,
  "is_repeated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has repeated the level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("progress_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_progress_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_progress_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "game_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the game the analytics pertain to */,
  "total_attempts" INTEGER /* Total attempts across all levels in the game */,
  "total_wins" INTEGER /* Total wins across all levels in the game */,
  "average_p_win" REAL /* Average win percentage across all levels in the game */,
  "total_time_spent" INTEGER /* Total time (in minutes) spent by all players on the game */,
  "average_time_spent_per_player" REAL /* Average time (in minutes) spent per player */,
  "total_errors" INTEGER /* Total number of errors encountered across all levels */,
  "average_errors_per_player" REAL /* Average number of errors per player */,
  "most_popular_level" INTEGER /* ID of the most popular level (most attempts) */,
  "least_popular_level" INTEGER /* ID of the least popular level (fewest attempts) */,
  "player_segment_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of player segments (e.g., 40% novice, 30% intermediate, 30% advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_game_id FOREIGN KEY ("game_id") REFERENCES games ("game_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user encountering the error */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* ID of the level where the error occurred */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message describing the encountered issue */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the error occurred */,
  "error_type" TEXT /* Type of error (e.g., connection, crash, lag) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the error has been resolved */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the error was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on the resolution of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES levels ("level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
